original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Vitamin B12 deficiency in over 16s: diagnosis and management
original_registry_entry.population: People aged 16 years and older with suspected or confirmed vitamin B12 deficiency, with pregnancy, breastfeeding, medicine, surgery and cause-specific qualifiers.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist assessment, testing, cause evaluation, ongoing care and follow-up.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for selected symptom, risk-factor, result-context, medicine-context and follow-up documentation. It does not infer deficiency, select treatment or establish UAE laboratory thresholds or pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.6 and Boxes 1–2 — recognition, symptoms and risk factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports variable and nonspecific symptom context, neurological, cognitive, visual, fatigue, anaemia, diet, medicine, surgery and other risk-factor documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.16 — tests, result context and interpretation factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording the type of vitamin B12 test, pregnancy or nitrous-oxide context, current supplements, laboratory reference context, indeterminate results and clinician-led further assessment without autonomously interpreting a result.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.6 — identifying cause
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented autoimmune-gastritis, coeliac, medicine, diet, surgery and other cause-review context without asserting a cause.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.14 — ongoing care and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports symptom response, worsening or new symptoms, adherence context and clinician-agreed reassessment or follow-up. Medicine route, dose and frequency are excluded from mappings.

title: Vitamin B12 deficiency in over 16s: diagnosis and management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: People aged 16 years and older with suspected or confirmed vitamin B12 deficiency, with pregnancy, breastfeeding, medicine, surgery and cause-specific qualifiers.
intended_setting: Primary and specialist assessment, testing, cause evaluation, ongoing care and follow-up.
